title: Smoke Test Report
description: Structured output from the harness smoke-test agent. Validates harness health, captures screenshots, checks console logs, and provides an honest retrospective.

health.description: Harness health check results from `just harness health`.

services.description: Per-service health status. Keys are service names (app, mcp, terminal, cdp), values are status strings.

path.description: File path where the screenshot was saved.

description.description: Optional description of what the screenshot shows.

consoleErrors.description: Browser console errors and warnings captured via CDP. Empty array if none found or if console check was skipped.

source.description: Source of the message (e.g., URL, script name).

serverLogSummary.description: Summary of server/container logs. Note any errors or warnings found.

verdict.description: Overall test verdict. 'pass' = all healthy, no console errors. 'fail' = health issues. 'partial' = healthy but with warnings.

retrospective.description: Honest feedback on the harness experience. This is the most valuable part of the report.

workedWell.description: What CLI commands were intuitive and worked well? What was pleasant about the harness experience?

confusing.description: What was confusing, unclear, or required trial-and-error? What information was hard to discover?

magicWand.description: If you could add or change one thing about the harness, what would it be? Be concrete.

cliDiscoverability.description: How discoverable were the CLI commands? Which did you find via --help? Which did you wish existed?

improvementSuggestions.description: 1-3 specific, actionable improvements for the harness CLI or documentation.
